description: Fix documentation inaccuracies and gaps found during systematic audit of all docs pages against the current source code. Covers incorrect secret/config descriptions in CLI reference, missing SDK utility functions in the utilities page, and missing WebSocket protocol messages in the MCP server reference.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Fix secret/config inaccuracies in CLI reference page
userStories.description: As a plugin developer reading the CLI docs, I want accurate descriptions of where the secret is stored and what config reset does, so that I don't get confused about the auth architecture.
acceptanceCriteria: The `opentabs start` first-run description no longer says 'Creates config.json with a generated secret' — instead it accurately describes that config.json is created for plugin/tool settings and auth.json is created with the generated secret in the extension directory
The `config reset` description no longer implies the secret is lost — it accurately says config.json is deleted (local plugins, tool overrides, permissions) but auth.json (the secret) is not affected

The `config rotate-secret` description no longer says the Chrome extension must be manually reloaded — it accurately says the server notifies the extension via POST /reload and the extension auto-reconnects with the new secret, so the transition is seamless for the extension
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx

Three fixes needed:

1. Lines ~70-74: The `opentabs start` section says 'Creates ~/.opentabs/config.json with a generated secret'. The secret is NOT in config.json — it's in ~/.opentabs/extension/auth.json. Change step 1 to something like: 'Creates ~/.opentabs/config.json (plugin and tool settings)' and add a step: 'Generates authentication secret in ~/.opentabs/extension/auth.json'.

2. Lines ~645-646: The `config reset` section says 'The server regenerates a fresh config (with a new secret) on the next opentabs start'. This is misleading — config reset only deletes config.json, it does NOT touch auth.json. The secret survives a config reset. Change to: 'The server regenerates a fresh config on the next opentabs start. The authentication secret in auth.json is not affected.'

3. Lines ~695: The `config rotate-secret` section says 'The Chrome extension must be reloaded to pick up the new secret from auth.json'. This is wrong — the server sends POST /reload with the old secret, the extension is notified via the WebSocket, and it auto-reconnects with the new secret. Change to explain the seamless transition. Remove the instruction to manually reload the extension. The note about MCP clients needing to update their configuration is correct and should stay.

userStories.title: Add missing SDK utility functions to the utilities reference page
userStories.description: As a plugin developer using the SDK, I want all exported utility functions documented so that I can discover and use them without reading source code.
acceptanceCriteria: The DOM Utilities section documents `waitForSelectorRemoval(selector, opts?)` with its signature (selector: string, opts?: WaitForSelectorOptions) and behavior (waits for element removal, resolves immediately if no matching element exists)

The DOM Utilities section documents `observeDOM(selector, callback, options?)` with its signature, ObserveDOMOptions interface (childList default true, attributes default false, subtree default true), return type (cleanup function), and error behavior (throws if element not found)

The Storage Utilities section documents `setLocalStorage(key, value)`, `removeLocalStorage(key)`, `setSessionStorage(key, value)`, `removeSessionStorage(key)` — all with their signatures and behavior (silently fail on SecurityError/QuotaExceededError)
The Fetch Utilities section documents `httpStatusToToolError(response, message)` with its mapping (401/403→auth, 404→not_found, 429→rate_limit with Retry-After, 5xx→internal)
The Fetch Utilities section documents `parseRetryAfterMs(value)` with its behavior (parses Retry-After header in seconds or HTTP-date format to milliseconds)
All new entries follow the existing documentation pattern — signature code block, description table or paragraph, consistent formatting
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/sdk/utilities.mdx

This page currently documents: fetchFromPage, fetchJSON, postJSON, putJSON, patchJSON, deleteJSON, postForm, postFormData (Fetch); waitForSelector, querySelectorAll, getTextContent (DOM); retry, waitUntil, sleep (Timing); getLocalStorage, getSessionStorage, getCookie (Storage); getPageGlobal, getCurrentUrl, getPageTitle (Page State); log namespace (Logging).

Missing functions that ARE exported from platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts:

DOM (add after getTextContent):
- waitForSelectorRemoval<T>(selector: string, opts?: WaitForSelectorOptions): Promise<void> — waits for element to be removed from DOM using MutationObserver. Resolves immediately if no matching element exists.
- observeDOM(selector: string, callback: (mutations: MutationRecord[], observer: MutationObserver) => void, options?: ObserveDOMOptions): (() => void) — sets up MutationObserver on matching element. Returns cleanup function. Throws if element not found. ObserveDOMOptions: { childList?: boolean (default true), attributes?: boolean (default false), subtree?: boolean (default true) }

Storage (add after getCookie):
- setLocalStorage(key: string, value: string): void — wraps localStorage.setItem(). Silently fails on SecurityError or QuotaExceededError.
- removeLocalStorage(key: string): void — wraps localStorage.removeItem(). Silently fails on SecurityError.
- setSessionStorage(key: string, value: string): void — wraps sessionStorage.setItem(). Silently fails on SecurityError or QuotaExceededError.
- removeSessionStorage(key: string): void — wraps sessionStorage.removeItem(). Silently fails on SecurityError.

Fetch (add after the existing fetch helpers section):
- httpStatusToToolError(response: Response, message: string): ToolError — maps HTTP status to appropriate ToolError. 401/403→auth (not retryable), 404→not_found (not retryable), 429→rate_limit (retryable, parses Retry-After), 5xx→internal (retryable, 503 parses Retry-After).
- parseRetryAfterMs(value: string): number | undefined — parses Retry-After header (seconds or HTTP-date format) to milliseconds.

Follow the existing formatting pattern: ### heading with function name, TypeScript signature code block, description, and parameter table where appropriate.

userStories.title: Add missing WebSocket protocol messages to MCP server reference
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want the MCP server WebSocket protocol documentation to include all extension→server messages so that I have a complete protocol reference.
acceptanceCriteria: The Extension → Server table includes `plugin.search` (Request — search npm registry for plugins, returns results array)
The Extension → Server table includes `plugin.install` (Request — install plugin from npm, returns ok/error)
The Extension → Server table includes `plugin.updateFromRegistry` (Request — update installed plugin to latest version, returns ok/oldVersion/newVersion/error)
The Extension → Server table includes `plugin.remove` (Request — remove an installed plugin, returns ok/error)
The Extension → Server table includes `plugin.checkUpdates` (Request — check for available plugin updates, returns available array with name/currentVersion/latestVersion)
All five messages are described as 'Request' type (they require an id and return responses)
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx

The Extension → Server WebSocket protocol table (lines ~428-488) is missing five plugin management messages that are handled by the MCP server. These are JSON-RPC requests sent from the extension's side panel to the server for plugin management.

Add these rows to the existing Extension → Server table:

| `plugin.search` | Request | Search npm registry for OpenTabs plugins. Params: `{ query }`. Response: `{ results: [{ name, version, description }] }`. |
| `plugin.install` | Request | Install a plugin from npm globally. Params: `{ name, version? }`. Response: `{ ok, error? }`. |
| `plugin.updateFromRegistry` | Request | Update an installed plugin to its latest npm version. Params: `{ name }`. Response: `{ ok, oldVersion, newVersion, error? }`. |
| `plugin.remove` | Request | Remove an installed plugin. Params: `{ name }`. Response: `{ ok, error? }`. |
| `plugin.checkUpdates` | Request | Check for available plugin updates. Params: none. Response: `{ available: [{ name, currentVersion, latestVersion }] }`. |

Group them together under the existing table, perhaps after the `config.*` messages. These are used by the Chrome extension's side panel to manage plugins without using the CLI.

Source code reference: platform/mcp-server/src/extension-protocol.ts lines 400-423, platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts for handler implementations.
